title: Mioca — the AI that keeps your business running (coming soon)
description: Mioca reads the market, proposes the next move, builds the deliverable, waits for your approval, ships, measures the result, and rewrites itself — running the loop unattended. Waitlist opening soon.
ogTitle: Mioca — a business that runs itself
ogDescription: From proposing to shipping, measuring, and revising. Let go, and the business keeps moving.
eyebrow: Mioca · the AI that keeps a business running
headline:
Let go of the wheel,
the business keeps moving.
lead: Mioca reads the market, proposes the next move, builds the deliverable, waits for your approval, ships it, measures what happened, and rewrites itself. You set the direction and approve the few calls that matter. The machine runs the rest.
waitlistNote: Not for sale yet. There's no checkout on this page. Join the waitlist and we'll tell you first when it opens — and share indicative pricing before anyone else.

caps.title: Decides the next move
caps.body: It reads data on the market and on what's working, then proposes what to do next. It forms a hypothesis and acts — it doesn't wait to be told.

caps.title: Builds, ships, measures
caps.body: It generates the deliverables — articles, landing pages, posts — ships them after approval, and measures reach and clicks to inform the next call.

caps.title: Sees the result, rewrites itself
caps.body: What worked and what didn't goes into a ledger, and the strategy itself gets rewritten. Not "learning" — revision. A little sharper each round.

how: Read the market and the response
Propose the next move — with a hypothesis
Wait for your approval on the calls that matter
Measure the result and record it
Rewrite the strategy, then go again

proof.heading: You can check the work, after the fact
proof.body: Because it runs on its own, it doesn't hide what it did. Every decision and deliverable is signed and logged, so you can verify it later. When it can't do something, it says so.
points: Decisions and shipments are logged — when, what, and why
Deliverables are signed, so tampering is detectable
Deterministic steps return the same output for the same input
Low-confidence output is held for a human, not published

pricing.note: Pricing is still being set. Waitlist members get indicative pricing before it's finalized. You can't buy or pay on this page yet.

faq.a: The date is still being set. Waitlist members hear about the launch and pricing first.
You set the direction and approve the calls that matter. Everything else — proposing, building, shipping, measuring, revising — the machine runs itself. We only claim what it can actually do.
Decide what to aim for, and approve high-risk actions. It isn't built around manual selling or hand-posting.

faq.q: How does this relate to the existing Mioca?
faq.a: Those are among the things Mioca produces. This OS runs the producing, shipping, and improving itself.

closingBody: We'll tell you first when it opens. Indicative pricing goes to the waitlist first, too.
